{
  "claims": [
    "Senate Bill 276 would require the California Department of Public Health to review medical exemptions submitted and those issued by physicians who submit a large number of exemptions.",
    "Senate Bill 276 reduces a parent’s right to decide what is best for their own children.",
    "Senate Bill 276 places parental decision-making in the hands of a government bureaucracy.",
    "Senate Bill 276 is described as a dangerous intrusion on the doctor-patient relationship.",
    "There is a belief among some lawmakers that the legislation will unfairly target families with medically-fragile children.",
    "Republican Senator Jeff Stone stated that one-size-fits-all medical exemptions are not appropriate.",
    "Senator Stone suggested that the State Medical Board should be empowered to enforce medical exemption provisions."
  ]
}
